elements.name: vaadin-range-slider
elements.description:
`<vaadin-range-slider>` is a web component that represents a range slider
for selecting a subset of the given range.

```html
<vaadin-range-slider min="0" max="100" step="1"></vaadin-range-slider>
```

### Styling

The following shadow DOM parts are available for styling:

Part name            | Description
---------------------|-----------------
`label`              | The label element
`required-indicator` | The required indicator element
`helper-text`        | The helper text element
`error-message`      | The error message element
`track`              | The slider track
`track-fill`         | The filled portion of the track
`thumb`              | The slider thumb (applies to both thumbs)
`thumb-start`        | The start (lower value) thumb
`thumb-end`          | The end (upper value) thumb
`marks`              | Container for min/max labels
`min`                | Minimum value label
`max`                | Maximum value label

The following state attributes are available for styling:

Attribute          | Description
-------------------|-------------
`disabled`         | Set when the slider is disabled
`readonly`         | Set when the slider is read-only
`focused`          | Set when the slider has focus
`focus-ring`       | Set when the slider is focused using the keyboard
`start-active`     | Set when the start thumb is activated with mouse or touch
`end-active`       | Set when the end thumb is activated with mouse or touch
`start-focused`    | Set when the start thumb has focus
`end-focused`      | Set when the end thumb has focus
`min-max-visible`  | Set when the min/max labels are displayed

The following custom CSS properties are available for styling:

Custom CSS property                          |
:--------------------------------------------|
`--vaadin-field-default-width`               |
`--vaadin-input-field-error-color`           |
`--vaadin-input-field-error-font-size`       |
`--vaadin-input-field-error-font-weight`     |
`--vaadin-input-field-helper-color`          |
`--vaadin-input-field-helper-font-size`      |
`--vaadin-input-field-helper-font-weight`    |
`--vaadin-input-field-label-color`           |
`--vaadin-input-field-label-font-size`       |
`--vaadin-input-field-label-font-weight`     |
`--vaadin-input-field-required-indicator`    |
`--vaadin-slider-bubble-arrow-size`          |
`--vaadin-slider-bubble-background`          |
`--vaadin-slider-bubble-border-color`        |
`--vaadin-slider-bubble-border-radius`       |
`--vaadin-slider-bubble-border-width`        |
`--vaadin-slider-bubble-offset`              |
`--vaadin-slider-bubble-padding`             |
`--vaadin-slider-bubble-shadow`              |
`--vaadin-slider-bubble-text-color`          |
`--vaadin-slider-bubble-font-size`           |
`--vaadin-slider-bubble-font-weight`         |
`--vaadin-slider-bubble-line-height`         |
`--vaadin-slider-fill-background`            |
`--vaadin-slider-fill-border-color`          |
`--vaadin-slider-fill-border-width`          |
`--vaadin-slider-marks-color`                |
`--vaadin-slider-marks-font-size`            |
`--vaadin-slider-marks-font-weight`          |
`--vaadin-slider-thumb-border-color`         |
`--vaadin-slider-thumb-border-radius`        |
`--vaadin-slider-thumb-border-width`         |
`--vaadin-slider-thumb-cursor`               |
`--vaadin-slider-thumb-cursor-active`        |
`--vaadin-slider-thumb-height`               |
`--vaadin-slider-thumb-width`                |
`--vaadin-slider-track-background`           |
`--vaadin-slider-track-border-color`         |
`--vaadin-slider-track-border-radius`        |
`--vaadin-slider-track-border-width`         |
`--vaadin-slider-track-height`               |

In order to style the slider bubble, use `<vaadin-slider-bubble>` shadow DOM parts:

Part name        | Description
-----------------|----------------------
`overlay`        | The overlay container
`content`        | The overlay content
`arrow`          | Arrow pointing to the thumb

See [Styling Components](https://vaadin.com/docs/latest/styling/styling-components) documentation.

attributes.name: accessible-name
attributes.description: String used to label the component to screen reader users.

attributes.name: accessible-name-end
attributes.description:
Custom accessible name for the end (maximum) input.
When not set, defaults to "${label} max" or "max" if no label.

attributes.name: accessible-name-ref
attributes.description: Id of the element used as label of the component to screen reader users.

attributes.name: accessible-name-start
attributes.description:
Custom accessible name for the start (minimum) input.
When not set, defaults to "${label} min" or "min" if no label.

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: If true, the user cannot interact with this element.

attributes.name: error-message
attributes.description: Error to show when the field is invalid.

attributes.name: helper-text
attributes.description: String used for the helper text.

attributes.name: invalid
attributes.description: Set to true when the field is invalid.

attributes.name: label
attributes.description:
The label text for the input node.
When no light dom defined via [slot=label], this value will be used.

attributes.name: manual-validation
attributes.description:
Set to true to enable manual validation mode. This mode disables automatic
constraint validation, allowing you to control the validation process yourself.
You can still trigger constraint validation manually with the `validate()` method
or use `checkValidity()` to assess the component's validity without affecting
the invalid state. In manual validation mode, you can also manipulate
the `invalid` property directly through your application logic without conflicts
with the component's internal validation.

attributes.name: max
attributes.description: The maximum allowed value.

attributes.name: min
attributes.description: The minimum allowed value.

attributes.name: min-max-visible
attributes.description: When true, displays the min and max values below the slider track.

attributes.name: readonly
attributes.description:
When true, the user cannot modify the value of the slider.
The difference between `disabled` and `readonly` is that the
read-only slider remains focusable and is announced by screen
readers.

attributes.name: required
attributes.description: Specifies that the user must fill in a value.

attributes.name: step
attributes.description: The stepping interval of the slider.

attributes.name: theme
attributes.description: The theme variants to apply to the component.

attributes.name: value-always-visible
attributes.description:
When true, the value bubble is always visible,
regardless of focus or hover state.

properties.name: accessibleName
properties.description: String used to label the component to screen reader users.

properties.name: accessibleNameEnd
properties.description:
Custom accessible name for the end (maximum) input.
When not set, defaults to "${label} max" or "max" if no label.

properties.name: accessibleNameRef
properties.description: Id of the element used as label of the component to screen reader users.

properties.name: accessibleNameStart
properties.description:
Custom accessible name for the start (minimum) input.
When not set, defaults to "${label} min" or "min" if no label.

properties.name: disabled
properties.description: If true, the user cannot interact with this element.

properties.name: errorMessage
properties.description: Error to show when the field is invalid.

properties.name: helperText
properties.description: String used for the helper text.

properties.name: invalid
properties.description: Set to true when the field is invalid.

properties.name: label
properties.description:
The label text for the input node.
When no light dom defined via [slot=label], this value will be used.

properties.name: manualValidation
properties.description:
Set to true to enable manual validation mode. This mode disables automatic
constraint validation, allowing you to control the validation process yourself.
You can still trigger constraint validation manually with the `validate()` method
or use `checkValidity()` to assess the component's validity without affecting
the invalid state. In manual validation mode, you can also manipulate
the `invalid` property directly through your application logic without conflicts
with the component's internal validation.

properties.name: max
properties.description: The maximum allowed value.

properties.name: min
properties.description: The minimum allowed value.

properties.name: minMaxVisible
properties.description: When true, displays the min and max values below the slider track.

properties.name: readonly
properties.description:
When true, the user cannot modify the value of the slider.
The difference between `disabled` and `readonly` is that the
read-only slider remains focusable and is announced by screen
readers.

properties.name: required
properties.description: Specifies that the user must fill in a value.

properties.name: step
properties.description: The stepping interval of the slider.

properties.name: value
properties.description: The value of the slider.

properties.name: valueAlwaysVisible
properties.description:
When true, the value bubble is always visible,
regardless of focus or hover state.

events.name: change
events.description: Fired when the user commits a value change.

events.name: input
events.description: Fired when the slider value changes during user interaction.

events.name: validated
events.description: Fired whenever the field is validated.

elements.name: vaadin-slider
elements.description:
`<vaadin-slider>` is a web component that represents a range slider
for selecting numerical values within a defined range.

```html
<vaadin-slider min="0" max="100" step="1"></vaadin-slider>
```

### Styling

The following shadow DOM parts are available for styling:

Part name            | Description
---------------------|-----------------
`label`              | The label element
`required-indicator` | The required indicator element
`helper-text`        | The helper text element
`error-message`      | The error message element
`track`              | The slider track
`track-fill`         | The filled portion of the track
`thumb`              | The slider thumb
`marks`              | Container for min/max labels
`min`                | Minimum value label
`max`                | Maximum value label

The following state attributes are available for styling:

Attribute          | Description
-------------------|-------------
`active`           | Set when the slider is activated with mouse or touch
`disabled`         | Set when the slider is disabled
`readonly`         | Set when the slider is read-only
`focused`          | Set when the slider has focus
`focus-ring`       | Set when the slider is focused using the keyboard
`min-max-visible`  | Set when the min/max labels are displayed

The following custom CSS properties are available for styling:

Custom CSS property                          |
:--------------------------------------------|
`--vaadin-field-default-width`               |
`--vaadin-input-field-error-color`           |
`--vaadin-input-field-error-font-size`       |
`--vaadin-input-field-error-font-weight`     |
`--vaadin-input-field-helper-color`          |
`--vaadin-input-field-helper-font-size`      |
`--vaadin-input-field-helper-font-weight`    |
`--vaadin-input-field-label-color`           |
`--vaadin-input-field-label-font-size`       |
`--vaadin-input-field-label-font-weight`     |
`--vaadin-input-field-required-indicator`    |
`--vaadin-slider-bubble-arrow-size`          |
`--vaadin-slider-bubble-background`          |
`--vaadin-slider-bubble-border-color`        |
`--vaadin-slider-bubble-border-radius`       |
`--vaadin-slider-bubble-border-width`        |
`--vaadin-slider-bubble-offset`              |
`--vaadin-slider-bubble-padding`             |
`--vaadin-slider-bubble-shadow`              |
`--vaadin-slider-bubble-text-color`          |
`--vaadin-slider-bubble-font-size`           |
`--vaadin-slider-bubble-font-weight`         |
`--vaadin-slider-bubble-line-height`         |
`--vaadin-slider-fill-background`            |
`--vaadin-slider-fill-border-color`          |
`--vaadin-slider-fill-border-width`          |
`--vaadin-slider-marks-color`                |
`--vaadin-slider-marks-font-size`            |
`--vaadin-slider-marks-font-weight`          |
`--vaadin-slider-thumb-border-color`         |
`--vaadin-slider-thumb-border-radius`        |
`--vaadin-slider-thumb-border-width`         |
`--vaadin-slider-thumb-cursor`               |
`--vaadin-slider-thumb-cursor-active`        |
`--vaadin-slider-thumb-height`               |
`--vaadin-slider-thumb-width`                |
`--vaadin-slider-track-background`           |
`--vaadin-slider-track-border-color`         |
`--vaadin-slider-track-border-radius`        |
`--vaadin-slider-track-border-width`         |
`--vaadin-slider-track-height`               |

In order to style the slider bubble, use `<vaadin-slider-bubble>` shadow DOM parts:

Part name        | Description
-----------------|----------------------
`overlay`        | The overlay container
`content`        | The overlay content
`arrow`          | Arrow pointing to the thumb

See [Styling Components](https://vaadin.com/docs/latest/styling/styling-components) documentation.
